_comment: FHIR R4 (HL7 Da Vinci PDEX Plan-Net) Provider Directory base URLs per Medi-Cal plan. CMS-9115-F requires the Provider Directory API to be PUBLIC (no authentication). The map uses a plan's endpoint as the primary in-network source of truth and only falls back to Google/OpenStreetMap when no endpoint is set or it returns nothing. baseUrl is filled ONLY for endpoints firsthand-verified to be live, public, and returning real FHIR data via Location?near geo search. Leave baseUrl empty when unconfirmed; those plans use the Tier-1 directory deep-link + public map data instead.

la-care.vendor: Edifecs HAPI FHIR R4 (server 5.4.1.13_edfx), Da Vinci Plan-Net style
la-care.notes: Public-no-auth confirmed (from lacare.org/technical-information). Locations carry NO position element, so geo=postal: query Location?address-postalcode (Bundle.total ~53 for one ZIP) and PractitionerRole?location.address-postalcode, then geocode the real street. address.line packs 'ORG - Service Type - STREET' and stamps a placeholder admin address (9230 W Olympic Blvd, Beverly Hills) on many records - cleanStreet/cleanOrgName + NPPES handle it. specialty.coding is usually NullFlavor UNK with the real specialty in specialty.text (specialtyText skips the UNK coding). Carries the Da Vinci 'newpatients' extension (acceptingPatients newpt/existptonly/nopt).

phc.notes: PHC California (Positive Healthcare/AHF) public no-auth FHIR PD on 1upHealth. No positions -> geocoded. Chained location.address-postalcode works. NPIs present.

iehp.notes: Open API, CapabilityStatement + live Practitioner query with real NPIs verified. CORS enabled.

health-net.notes: FHIR base URL is portal-gated, so we use Health Net's public per-county Medi-Cal provider-directory JSON, flattened + ZIP-centroid geocoded by scripts/build-healthnet.mjs into data/healthnet-providers.json. Pins are approximate to the provider's ZIP area (source has no per-record coordinates).

molina.notes: Public-no-auth verified live (Location total 1.4M, PractitionerRole 16M). Diagnosed 2026-06: individual-doctor retrieval is NOT possible here - `near` 500s, chained location.address-postalcode 500s, _include/_elements 500, and PractitionerRole?location=<id> returns 0 (no PR<->Location linkage). What DOES work is Location?address-postalcode per single ZIP, returning real positioned in-network LOCATIONS (e.g. 90011 -> 253). The earlier '~2 results' bug was two-fold: (1) downtown LA's nearest ZIP centroids are mostly PO-box/admin ZIPs holding 0 locations, and (2) a 'clinic' name-keyword filter dropped the rest. Fixed by querying a wide ZIP set (24, early-stop ~150, concurrency 4) and not name-filtering in-network locations. So Molina shows in-network LOCATIONS (practices/clinics) with exact coords, not named individual doctors. Locations carry real position lat/lng.

blue-shield-promise.notes: Public-no-auth production endpoint verified live (2026-06). `near` geo search works: ~96 providers within 15mi of downtown LA, but only ~1 within 10mi - BSC Promise's mapped provider positions near downtown are genuinely sparse (most sit 11-17mi out), so tight-radius counts are honestly low and grow with radius / a travel-time area. (Forcing a wider near radius then trimming by distance does NOT help and can time out, since the extra results are all beyond the trim distance.) Backend mdc = Medi-Cal directory cloud. Host api.blueshieldca.com, path /bsc/fhir/.

anthem.notes: metadata public at totalview.healthos.elevancehealth.com/.../AnthemBlueCross/api/v1/fhir but data queries 403 without an API key (request via InteroperabilityWorkgroup@Anthem.com).

gold-coast.notes: Public no-auth FHIR Provider Directory (discovered via fdp.edifecsfedcloud.com catalog). No positions -> geocoded. Chained location.address-postalcode works.

kaiser.notes: The kpx-service-bus.kp.org FHIR gateway is NOT usable publicly: it returns HTTP 406 to standard FHIR requests (even with Accept: application/fhir+json server-side) - it is registration/key gated like Anthem, not public no-auth as first thought (verified live: /api/innetwork?plan=kaiser returns reason no-providers). Kaiser is a CLOSED network, so a member's in-network facilities are exactly the Kaiser Permanente-branded ones. The client therefore plots Kaiser Permanente facilities from public map data (OpenStreetMap, real coordinates) and marks them in-network green - see closedNetworkBrand.

kern.notes: Only a member-consent Patient Access API evidenced; no public Provider Directory endpoint found.
